calibration_eval: Isotonic calibrator mapping s -> P(sign correct), CROSS-FIT across the 4 donor folds (fit on 3 folds' edges, apply to the 4th), pooled. Reliability diagram + Expected Calibration Error (10 equal-count bins).
confidence_score: s = |mean_calib log_fc| / (sd_calib log_fc + 0.05), a consensus-strength (t-like) score from the 3 calibration pairs ONLY. Higher s = more confident sign.

data.common_contrasts: 4879 (target_contrast x culture_condition) present in ALL 6 pairs — the aligned unit set.
data.layers_used: log_fc (signed downstream effect); adj_p_value only for descriptive base-rate reporting.
data.readout_panel: Downstream readout = the 2298 genes that are BOTH measured AND are themselves perturbation targets in the reproducible core (the regulatory network AMONG perturbed genes). Principled, symmetric 'downstream program', not arbitrary.
data.structure: 6 donor-pair modalities from 4 donors; each pair = perturbations reproducibly detected in BOTH its donors (reproducible core).

honesty_contract: Prereg frozen (sha256 over content, timestamp EXCLUDED) BEFORE any scoring. Success criteria stated a priori. An effect must SURVIVE the self-knockdown exclusion AND beat the constant-sign baseline to count. No tuning after seeing results. If NULL/trivial/underpowered, reported as such.

lodo_design.calibration: For held-out donor d: the 3 pairs whose BOTH members != d (pairs_excluding).
lodo_design.edge_selection_per_fold: Candidate edge iff median over the 3 CALIBRATION pairs of |log_fc| >= tau_mag (an effect exists to have a sign). tau_mag = 0.5 log2FC (primary); sensitivity at 0.25 and 1.0.
lodo_design.folds: 4 leave-one-donor-out folds (same as Lever A / conformal.py).
lodo_design.test: The 3 pairs containing d (pairs_containing). Exchangeability assertion: no calib pair contains d.

metrics: selective_accuracy MINUS constant_baseline at coverage=50% (primary lift), bootstrap 95% CI
ECE of the calibrated sign confidence
one_line: Can the trust layer be calibrated to trust the SIGN (up vs down) of a perturbation's downstream transcriptional effect on a defined readout, held-out across donors, with a valid abstention when the sign is uncertain — ABOVE the trivial/constant-sign baseline?
relation_to_prior: Lever A calibrated effect MAGNITUDE within-assay (AUROC 0.709). The gate currently trusts magnitude only. This attacks a named narrowness: it asks whether direction can be trusted with valid abstention. NOTE: distinct from day8_outcome_direction (that was clinical-trial drug success/failure); this is TRANSCRIPTIONAL sign of downstream regulation.
risk_coverage_significance: AURC-style: error = 1 - correct, trust = s; permutation null (2000 shuffles of s) for whether s orders sign-correctness better than random. Reuses conformal.aurc / aurc_permutation_test.
selective_prediction: Rank edges by s (pooled across the 4 folds). Sweep coverage from 100% down to 10%; at each coverage predict on the top-s fraction, abstain on the rest. Selective accuracy = sign-correct fraction among non-abstained. (Set-valued analog: {up},{down}, or {up,down}=ABSTAIN.)
sign_task_defined: For each edge, predict the SIGN (up=+1 / down=-1) of its downstream log_fc on the HELD-OUT donor, using ONLY calibration-donor evidence, with an ABSTENTION option. Prediction = sign(mean_calib log_fc). Ground-truth = sign(mean_test log_fc) on the held-out-donor pairs. This is calibrated cross-donor sign trust: output a calibrated confidence that the estimated sign will hold on an unseen donor, and abstain when it will not.

success_bar_a_priori.no_or_underpowered: Selective accuracy does not exceed the constant baseline, OR the lift CI includes 0, OR the effective edge/donor count is too small to resolve the bar. Reported plainly as NULL/underpowered per the honesty contract.
success_bar_a_priori.partial: Some but not all of the above (e.g. beats base rate but calibration weak, or lift positive but CI touches 0).
real_new_capability_YES_requires_ALL: At coverage=50%: (selective_accuracy - constant_majority_baseline) > 0 with bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 0 (paired over folds/bootstraps).
Risk-coverage permutation p < 0.05 (abstention genuinely orders sign-correctness).
ECE < 0.10 (confidence is calibrated) AND reliability non-degenerate.

title: Calibrated DIRECTION / SIGN trust — a pre-registered scope expansion of the CD4+ Perturb-seq commit-gate (T1D)
trivial_guard: (1) SELF-KNOCKDOWN EXCLUDED: every edge with g_j == g_i is removed (self-knockdown sign is ~always down = the trivial artifact). (2) CONSTANT-SIGN BASELINE: on the exact same non-abstained edge set at every coverage, compare against the majority-sign constant classifier accuracy = max(p_up, p_down). A win requires BEATING this on non-trivial downstream edges. (3) Edge universe selected using CALIBRATION donors ONLY (no held-out leakage). (4) Base rate (overall sign-correctness at full coverage) and constant-baseline reported first-class.
unit_of_analysis: An EDGE = (contrast row c = perturbed target g_i under a culture condition, downstream readout gene g_j), with g_j in the 2298-gene panel.
